other_observers: Brenda Linfield; I was not the original finder of this bird. It was previously reported on cobirds.
description: It was a large, white owl, sitting on a fence post in open grassland.  It had a clean white face, throat, and central upper breast. The white on the breast had a "V" shape.  It had a dusky nape, with prominently barred wings, flanks, and belly.  It had a darker gray or black tail, as seen when the wings were folded.  The eyebrows were obvious (i.e. darker than the face).  It had white, heavily feathered feet, with short legs.Behaviors: During my observation, it was sitting on a fence post in an open field.Call: none heardPlumage: adult female or immature
